get.summary: Parameters queries the parameters of the module.

AggregateRoot returns the keccak256 Merkle root over all accepted attestations
(the value a relayer anchors on Ethereum), plus the leaf count and the chain
height the snapshot reflects.
GetAttestation retrieves an attestation by username and nullifier.

MerkleProof returns the inclusion proof for the attestation of a given content
hash, against the current aggregate root — used to verify settlement on L1.
IsNullifierUsed checks whether a nullifier has been used before.
Parameters queries the parameters of the module.

VerifyContent checks the origin of a piece of content by its hash. Given
SHA-256(content) (hex), it returns every accepted human-typing attestation
bound to that content. This is the public provenance-verification endpoint:
a match proves the text was typed by a human on a registered Sur device.
GetDeviceCommitment queries a device commitment for a user at a given index.
Parameters queries the parameters of the module.
GetUserProfile queries a user profile by username.
Parameters queries the parameters of the module.

surchain.attestation.v1.AttestationMatch.description: AttestationMatch is one attestation bound to the queried content.

origin.description: Provenance class and optional citation (see AttestationRecord).

surchain.attestation.v1.MsgSubmitAttesta.description: MsgSubmitAttestation submits a ZK-proven content attestation.

citation.description: Optional citation for "external_source" / "imported" (e.g. a URL, book, note).

device_pubkey.description:
Compressed secp256k1 device public key (must hash to the device id in
`username`). Required for declaration origins; unused for human_keyboard.

device_signature.description:
secp256k1 signature over SHA-256(content_hash || origin || citation || nullifier),
proving the device made this declaration. Required for declaration origins.

origin.description:
Provenance class of the content. Empty or "human_keyboard" => a Sur Keyboard
human-typing attestation backed by the Groth16 proof above. Other values
("device_authored", "ai_generated", "external_source", "imported") are
device-signed DECLARATIONS — they do NOT assert human typing and are secured
by the device signature below instead of the ZK proof.

surchain.attestation.v1.MsgSubmitAttesta.description: MsgSubmitAttestationResponse defines the response for SubmitAttestation.

surchain.attestation.v1.MsgUpdateParams.description: MsgUpdateParams is the Msg/UpdateParams request type.

authority.description: authority is the address that controls the module (defaults to x/gov unless overwritten).

params.description:
params defines the module parameters to update.

NOTE: All parameters must be supplied.

surchain.attestation.v1.MsgUpdateParamsR.description:
MsgUpdateParamsResponse defines the response structure for executing a
MsgUpdateParams message.

surchain.attestation.v1.Params.description: Params defines the parameters for the module.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryAggregateRo.description:
QueryAggregateRootResponse carries the keccak Merkle root over all accepted
attestations, the number of leaves, and the chain height it reflects.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryGetAttestat.description: QueryGetAttestationResponse is the response for GetAttestation.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryIsNullifier.description: QueryIsNullifierUsedResponse is the response for IsNullifierUsed.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryMerkleProof.description: QueryMerkleProofResponse carries an inclusion proof against the aggregate root.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryParamsRespo.description: QueryParamsResponse is response type for the Query/Params RPC method.

params.description: params holds all the parameters of this module.

surchain.attestation.v1.QueryVerifyConte.description:
QueryVerifyContentResponse is the response for VerifyContent. found is true
when at least one attestation exists for the content; attestations lists them
all (the same text can be attested by multiple users/sessions).
